text: ‘Thy angel’; in the Hebrew text, ‘a rumour’.
‘Of ours’; literally, ‘of thine’, the prophet addressing (it seems) an imaginary contemporary of his own.

‘The men we sent out to report’; literally, ‘those who see’, but it does not seem probable that the phrase refers here, as it often does, to the prophets. The Hebrew text differs, and its meaning is much disputed.
According to the Hebrew text, the prophet asks what has become of the notaries, the men who weighed (out money, to buy off Assyria), the men who counted the towers of Jerusalem.
‘A rebellious people’; this word, nowhere else found, is understood by some modern scholars in the sense of ‘barbarous’, and the whole verse is taken as referring to the Assyrians.
